title: Google to Officially Power Apple AI Siri

mentions.quoted_text: Apple and Google have entered into a multi-year collaboration under which the next generation of Apple Foundation models will be based on Google's Gemini models and cloud technology.
mentions.context_snippet: Apple Foundation models will be based on Google's Gemini models and cloud technology.

facts.fact_value: Gemini models will power future Apple Intelligence features including a more personalized Siri.
Gemini leads in multimodality and is better at using cameras than OpenAI's models.

mentions.quoted_text: Apple is finally going to fix Siri, and more broadly, it seems, maybe take AI seriously, and they're going to do so with Google as their partner.
mentions.context_snippet: Apple is finally going to fix Siri with Google powering its AI models.

facts.fact_value: Siri will be upgraded with AI powered by Google's Gemini models.

mentions.quoted_text: Anthropic announced the launch of Clawed for Healthcare.
mentions.context_snippet: Clawed is used by Anthropic for healthcare and life sciences applications.

facts.fact_value: Clawed for Healthcare provides HIPAA-ready products and connectors for healthcare databases.

mentions.quoted_text: Elon Musk posted about a major upgrade to Grok code coming next month.
mentions.context_snippet: Grok code will one-shot many complex coding tasks after upgrade.

facts.fact_value: Grok code will have a major upgrade enabling one-shot complex coding tasks.

mentions.quoted_text: Anthropic's crackdown affected popular open-source coding agent OpenCode.
mentions.context_snippet: OpenCode was affected by Anthropic's new technical controls to prevent spoofing of Clawed code.

facts.fact_value: OpenCode's access to Anthropic models was restricted due to new technical controls.

mentions.quoted_text: Google has launched their new agentic shopping standard called the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP).
mentions.context_snippet: UCP is an open-source standard for shopping agents to gather product info and navigate checkout.

facts.fact_value: UCP is an open-source, non-proprietary protocol developed by Google and retail partners for agentic shopping interoperability.

notes: Mentions of organizations like The Guardian, Reuters, Bloomberg, and The Verge are included only when central to specific claims or attribution.
Some persons mentioned are included due to their quoted commentary or leadership roles relevant to the claims.
URLs are included only when explicitly provided or clearly known from the transcript.
No URLs were invented; source_url is null when unknown.
Sentiment labels are mostly neutral or positive given the mostly factual or optimistic tone of the references.
Editorial flag is true for host commentary and news analysis; false for sponsor reads.
Mentions are capped at 40 to maintain quality and relevance.
